SEARCH
Package dev.jeka.core.api.system

Class JkProperties

java.lang.Object
dev.jeka.core.api.system.JkProperties
public final class JkProperties extends Object
A set of name-value pairs. Names and values are typed as String.

This object provides interpolation and fallback features.

Part of the value can be expressed by referencing the name of another property. For example, if we have 

         foo=bar
         foo2=${foo}${foo}
then foo2=barbar

JkProperties objects can be combined to form a chain of resolution using a fallback mechanism.

  • Field Details

    • SENSITIVE_KEY_PATTERN

      public static final Predicate<String> SENSITIVE_KEY_PATTERN
      A predicate used to determine if a given string is a sensitive key. A sensitive key is determined by its ending, which could be "password", "secret", "token", or "pwd". Case-insensitive comparison is used.

    • ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLES

      public static final JkProperties ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLES
      Environment variables exposed as JkProperties.

      Each environment variable is exposed with 2 names :

      • Its original name as it is known by the OS (e.g MY_ENV_VAR)
      • Its dot-lowercase name counterpart (e.g. my.env.var)

    • EMPTY

      public static final JkProperties EMPTY

  • Method Details

    • ofMap

      public static JkProperties ofMap(String sourceName, Map<String,String> props)

      public static JkProperties ofMap(Map<String,String> props)

      public static JkProperties ofSysPropsThenEnv()

    • ofStandardProperties

      public static JkProperties ofStandardProperties()
      Returns an instance of JkProperties from system properties and environment variables, with fallback to global.properties if they exist.

    • ofFile

      public static JkProperties ofFile(Path propertyFile)
      Returns an instance of JkProperties by loading properties from the specified file.

    • withFallback

      public JkProperties withFallback(JkProperties fallback)
      Returns a JkProperties instance from this one plus the specified fallback.

    • sub

      public JkProperties sub(String prefix)
      Creates a new JkProperties instance containing only the properties with keys that start with the specified prefix. The prefix is removed from the keys in the resulting properties.
      Parameters:
      prefix - the prefix to filter the properties by; only properties with keys starting with this prefix will be included
      Returns:
      a new JkProperties instance with filtered properties based on the specified prefix

    • get

      public String get(String propertyName)
      Returns the value associated with the specified property name or null if no value has been specified for this name.

      See Environment variable conventions

    • getNonBlank

      public String getNonBlank(String propertyName)
      Retrieves the value associated with the specified property name, returning the value if non-blank, or null if the value is blank or not specified.
      Parameters:
      propertyName - the name of the property to retrieve
      Returns:
      the value associated with the specified property name if non-blank, or null otherwise

    • getTrimmedNonBlank

      public String getTrimmedNonBlank(String propertyName)
      Retrieves the value associated with the specified property name, trims it, and returns it if non-blank. If the value is blank or not specified, returns null.
      Parameters:
      propertyName - the name of the property to retrieve
      Returns:
      the trimmed property value if it is non-blank, or null otherwise

    • get

      public String get(String propertyName, String defaultValue)
      Returns the value associated with the specified property name or the defaultValue if no value has been specified for this name.

    • containsKey

      public boolean containsKey(String propName)
      Checks if the given property name exists in the properties.

    • getAll

      public Map<String,String> getAll()

    • getAllStartingWith

      public Map<String,String> getAllStartingWith(String prefix, boolean keepPrefix)

    • find

      public Set<String> find(String prefix)
      Returns all keys with the specified prefix. If prefix is null or empty, then returns all keys.

    • getSource

      public String getSource()
      Returns the source of the properties, it can be a file, system-properties or a simple map

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object

    • toColumnText

      public JkColumnText toColumnText(int keyMaxLength, int valueMaxLength, boolean canTruncate)
      Converts the properties to a formatted column text representation.
      Parameters:
      keyMaxLength - The maximum length of the key column.
      valueMaxLength - The maximum length of the value column.
      canTruncate - Flag indicating whether the values can be truncated if they exceed the maximum length.
